# -*- sh -*-
# See http://rigaux.org/language-study/diagram.html for the format of this file
#claude: auto-generated by Claude Code (inspired from my other lineage.txt)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Debuggers lineage
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

#src: wikipedia articles on different programs
#src: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Debugger
#src: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_debuggers

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-Historical
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# the term "debugging" predates computers (Grace Hopper, 1947 moth story)
# early debugging was done with front panel switches and core dumps

*DDT
  1961
  # Dynamic Debugging Technique, MIT, PDP-1
  # first interactive debugger, introduced breakpoints

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (UNIX)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*db (UNIX)
  1971
  DDT
  SY:2
  # Ken Thompson, original UNIX debugger
  # part of First Edition UNIX, PDP-11

*adb
  1979
  db (UNIX)
  SY:2
  # Steve Bourne, UNIX V7, assembly-level debugger
  # "advanced debugger", terse command syntax

*sdb
  1983
  adb
  SY:2
  # UNIX System V, source-level debugger
  # first UNIX debugger to support C source-level debugging

*dbx
  1983
  adb
  SY:2
  # BSD, Mark Linton (also InterViews author)
  # source-level, symbolic debugger, used on BSD/Sun/SGI

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (GNU)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*gdb
  1986
  dbx
  SY:2
  # Richard Stallman, GNU Debugger
  # supports many languages and architectures
*gdb 4.0
  1991
  # remote debugging
*gdb 5.0
  2000
  # GDB/MI machine interface
*gdb 7.0
  2009
  # reversible debugging, Python scripting

*LLDB
  2010
  gdb
  SY:2
  # LLVM project debugger, uses Clang AST for expression evaluation
  # default debugger on macOS/Xcode

*Purify
  1991
  SY:6
  # Reed Hastings (yes, Netflix founder), Pure Software
  # pioneering commercial memory error detector

*Valgrind
  2002
  Purify
  SY:2
  # Julian Seward, free replacement for Purify
  # Memcheck is the most used tool

*strace
  1991
  SY:2
  # system call tracer for Linux
  # originally by Paul Kranenburg for SunOS (trace)

ltrace
  1997
  strace
  # library call tracer

*rr
  2014
  gdb
  # Mozilla, record-and-replay debugger
  # deterministic replay, very powerful for hard bugs

UndoDB
  2005
  gdb
  SY:6
  # Undo Ltd, commercial reversible debugger

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plan9
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*db
  1993
  adb
  SY:1
  # Plan 9 debugger, like adb but for Plan 9 binary format

*acid
  1994
  db
  SY:1
  # Plan 9, Phil Winterbottom, programmable debugger
  # Acid language for writing debugging scripts

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ------------
# Microsoft
# ------------

*Microsoft CodeView
  1985
  SY:6
  # ad for Turbo compares itself with CodeView 2.2

*Visual Studio Debugger
  1997
  Microsoft CodeView
  SY:6
  # integrated in Visual Studio, replaced CodeView

WinDbg
  1999
  SY:6
  # Windows kernel and user-mode debugger
  # part of Debugging Tools for Windows

# ------------
# Borland
# ------------

*Turbo Debugger
  1988
  SY:6
  # ad in infoworld for Turbo Debugger 1.0 sep 12 1988
  # excellent TUI, integrated with Turbo C/Pascal

# ------------
# Other industry
# ------------

D86
  1986
  SY:6
D386
  1989
  SY:6
  # from A86/A386 author (Eric Isaacson)

*SoftICE
  1987
  SY:6
  # NuMega, kernel-level debugger for DOS/Windows
  # legendary tool for reverse engineering

TotalView
  1990
  SY:6
  # Thinking Machines, then Rogue Wave
  # parallel/HPC debugger, MPI support

*DDD
  1996
  gdb
  # Data Display Debugger, graphical frontend for gdb
  # visualizes data structures as graphs

Lauterbach TRACE32
  1985
  SY:6
  # embedded/JTAG debugger, dominant in embedded industry

*Pernosco
  2017
  rr
  SY:6
  # cloud-based omniscient debugger built on rr

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Academic / Research
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*SML debugger
  1990
  # Andrew Tolmach and Andrew Appel, time-travel debugging for SML/NJ
  # pioneered reverse execution in a high-level language

*ocamldebug
  1995
  SML debugger
  # Caml Light/OCaml debugger with time-travel (checkpointing via fork)

*DUEL
  1993
  gdb
  # Michael Golan, debugging expression language for gdb
  # query-like expressions to explore data structures

*ODB
  2003
  # Bil Lewis, Omniscient Debugger for Java
  # recorded entire execution history, step backwards through time

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exotic / Specialized
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Electric Fence
  1993
  # Bruce Perens, malloc debugger using mmap/mprotect

*AddressSanitizer (ASan)
  2012
  # Google, compile-time memory error detection
  # faster than Valgrind, integrated in gcc/clang

*KGDB
  2001
  gdb
  # Linux kernel debugger, remote gdb stub in the kernel

crash
  1999
  # Dave Anderson, Linux kernel crash dump analyzer

*DTrace
  2005
  # Sun Microsystems, dynamic tracing framework
  # Bryan Cantrill, for Solaris, later ported to macOS/FreeBSD

*SystemTap
  2005
  DTrace
  # Red Hat, Linux equivalent of DTrace
  # tapsets, kprobes-based

*BPF/bpftrace
  2018
  DTrace
  # modern Linux tracing, eBPF-based
  # Brendan Gregg popularized it
